# Load the JSON files
import json, sys, os, re, math
pth1 = "/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json"
pth2 = "/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json"
data_q = json.load(open(pth1, "r", encoding="utf-8"))
data_h = json.load(open(pth2, "r", encoding="utf-8"))
len(data_q), len(data_h)

# Inspect a quranic verse entry
for i in range(0,5):
    print(i, list(data_q[i].keys()))
    print(data_q[i]['ayah_text'][:150])
    print("-----")

response_context = """الإجابة على السؤال هي أن الكهف الذي أوى إليه فتية الكهف يقع في بلدة رقرقة، التي تقع في محافظة الشام (حالياً محافظة درعا في سوريا).
قال الله تعالى في القرآن الكريم:
"وَإِخْوَةُ الْكَهْفِ وَإِذْ تَقَوَّمُوا عَلَى أَهْلِهِمْ فَأَحَصْنَهُمْ فَأَفَخَطَتْ بِهِمُ الْغَارُ وَظَنَوا أَنَّ رَبَّهُمْ فَخِذٌ وَظَنَ النَّبِيُّ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ بِمَضْجَعِهِمْ وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ عَقِلُوا لَأَمَسُّوا بِأَيْدِيهِمْ وَذُوقُوا الْعَذَابَ مِنَ الْغَارِ وَلَقَدْ أَمْلَأْنَا بَيْنَهُمْ مِنْ بَيْنٍ" (سورة الكهف، الآية 15)
وقال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم:
"إِنَّ أَخَوانِ الْكَهْفِ لَم يُفَنَّ أَعقابُهُمَا وَلَوْ كُنَّا مُحَدِّثِينَ لَم يُؤَمَّنَ بِأَقاوِيلِنَا" (رواه البخاري)
وقال أيضاً:
"وَإِنَّ أَخَوانِ الْكَهْفِ لَم يُبَدَّدَ نُفوسُهُمَا" (رواه البخاري)
وفي رواية أخرى:
"وَإِنَّ أَخَوانِ الْكَهْفِ لَم يُفَنَّ أَعقابُهُمَا وَلَوْ كُنَّا مُحَدِّثِينَ لَم يُؤَمَّنَ بِأَقاوِيلِنَا وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ عَقِلُوا لَأَمَسُّوا بِأَيْدِيهِمْ وَذُوقُوا الْعَذَابَ مِنَ الْغَارِ" (رواه مسلم)
ومن الجدير بالذكر أن الكهف الذي أوى إليه فتية الكهف هو الكهف الذي وجدوه في جبل رقرقة، والذي كان يُسمى بالكهف المقدس."""
len(response_context), response_context[535:640]

# Normalization function for Arabic text
import re

# Arabic diacritics ranges
arabic_diacritics = re.compile(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u06D6-\u06ED\u0670\u06E1\u06E2\u06E3\u06E4\u06E7\u06E8\u06EA-\u06EF\u08E2]')
# tatweel
tatweel_re = re.compile(r'[\u0640]')
# punctuation
punct_re = re.compile(r'[^\w\s\u0621-\u064A\u0660-\u0669\u06F0-\u06F9]')  # remove non-word non-space non-Arabic letters/digits
# But \w includes underscore and digits etc. We'll create more precise removal: remove punctuation but keep Arabic letters and Arabic digits and spaces.

def normalize_arabic(text):
    text = str(text)
    # remove diacritics
    text = arabic_diacritics.sub('', text)
    # remove tatweel
    text = tatweel_re.sub('', text)
    # unify alef variants
    text = re.sub('[أإآٱ]', 'ا', text)
    # replace 'ى' with 'ي'
    text = text.replace('ى', 'ي')
    # unify hamza on waw/ya to bare hamza? replace 'ؤ' and 'ئ' with 'ء'
    text = text.replace('ؤ', 'ء').replace('ئ', 'ء')
    # remove punctuation except arabic letters and digits and space and hamza etc
    # keep Arabic letters \u0621-\u064A and Arabic-Indic digits \u0660-\u0669 and extended \u06F0-\u06F9 and space and ء
    text = re.sub(r'[^\u0621-\u064A\u0660-\u0669\u06F0-\u06F9\sء]', ' ', text)
    # collapse spaces
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text).strip()
    # lowercase: not applicable for Arabic but keep same
    return text

# Test normalization
orig = "إِنَّ أَخَوانِ الْكَهْفِ لَم يُفَنَّ أَعقابُهُمَا وَلَوْ كُنَّا مُحَدِّثِينَ لَم يُؤَمَّنَ بِأَقاوِيلِنَا"
print(orig)
print(normalize_arabic(orig))

# Implement similarity measures and search
from collections import Counter
def token_f1(a, b):
    # tokens as lists
    ta = a.split()
    tb = b.split()
    if len(ta)==0 or len(tb)==0:
        return 0.0
    ca = Counter(ta)
    cb = Counter(tb)
    overlap = sum(min(ca[t], cb[t]) for t in set(ta) & set(tb))
    if overlap==0:
        return 0.0
    prec = overlap / len(tb)
    rec = overlap / len(ta)
    f1 = 2*prec*rec/(prec+rec)
    return f1

def lcs_char_ratio(a, b):
    # longest common substring length / max(len(a), len(b))
    # dynamic programming using array
    la = len(a)
    lb = len(b)
    if la==0 or lb==0:
        return 0.0
    # For memory efficiency, use rolling array
    prev = [0]*(lb+1)
    longest = 0
    for i in range(1, la+1):
        curr = [0]*(lb+1)
        ca = a[i-1]
        for j in range(1, lb+1):
            if ca == b[j-1]:
                curr[j] = prev[j-1] + 1
                if curr[j] > longest:
                    longest = curr[j]
            else:
                curr[j] = 0
        prev = curr
    ratio = longest / max(la, lb)
    return ratio

# Compose normalized query strings
user_provided = "إِنَّ أَخَوانِ الْكَهْفِ لَم يُفَنَّ أَعقابُهُمَا وَلَوْ كُنَّا مُحَدِّثِينَ لَم يُؤَمَّنَ بِأَقاوِيلِنَا"
full_span = response_context[535:640]
print("full_span equals user_provided:", full_span == user_provided)

norm_user = normalize_arabic(user_provided)
norm_full = normalize_arabic(full_span)
norm_user, norm_full

# Search through hadith DB
candidates = []
q_norm = norm_full  # using full extracted substring
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    # get candidate matn/hadithTxt
    cand = ""
    if entry.get('Matn'):
        cand = entry['Matn']
    elif entry.get('hadithTxt'):
        cand = entry['hadithTxt']
    else:
        continue
    cand_norm = normalize_arabic(cand)
    # compute substring check quickly
    substring_match = (q_norm in cand_norm) or (cand_norm in q_norm)
    # compute token f1 and lcs ratios
    f1 = token_f1(q_norm, cand_norm)
    lcs = lcs_char_ratio(q_norm, cand_norm)
    score = max(f1, lcs)
    # only store those with reasonable score > 0.5 to save memory
    if score > 0.4 or substring_match:
        candidates.append((idx, score, f1, lcs, substring_match, cand[:200]))
        
# sort by score descending
candidates_sorted = sorted(candidates, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
len(candidates_sorted), candidates_sorted[:20]

# Search for occurrences of 'الكهف' in hadithDB normalized
target = normalize_arabic("الْكَهْفِ")
matches = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ""
    if "الكهف" in cand or "الكهفِ" in cand or "الْكَهْفِ" in cand:
        # record
        matches.append((idx, cand[:200]))
len(matches)

# Search normalized for 'اخوان' and 'اخوين' near 'الكهف'
search_terms = ['اخوان الكهف', 'اخوين الكهف', 'اخوان الكهف', 'اخوان الكهف لم', 'اخوان الكهف لم يفن']
target_norms = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ""
    cand_norm = normalize_arabic(cand)
    for st in search_terms:
        if st in cand_norm:
            target_norms.append((idx, st, cand[:200]))
            break

len(target_norms), target_norms[:20]

# Compute token f1 for all entries and get top 50
scores = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ""
    cand_norm = normalize_arabic(cand)
    f1 = token_f1(q_norm, cand_norm)
    # only store those with any overlap
    if f1>0:
        scores.append((idx, f1, cand[:200]))
# sort by f1 desc
scores_sorted = sorted(scores, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
len(scores_sorted), scores_sorted[:30]

# search for 'اعقاب' in Matn or hadithTxt raw
hits = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ""
    if 'أعقاب' in cand or 'اعقاب' in cand or 'أعقابُهُمَا' in cand or 'أعقابهما' in cand:
        hits.append((idx, cand[:200]))
len(hits), hits[:20]

# search for 'أخوين' or 'اخوين' or 'أخوان' 
hits2 = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ""
    if 'أخوين' in cand or 'اخوين' in cand or 'أخَوين' in cand or 'أَخَوَين' in cand:
        hits2.append((idx, cand[:400]))
len(hits2), hits2[:20]

# Search for normalized 'اخوان' occurrences in normalized candidates
term = 'اخوان'
hits_norm = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ""
    cand_norm = normalize_arabic(cand)
    if term in cand_norm:
        hits_norm.append((idx, cand[:200], cand_norm[:200]))
len(hits_norm), hits_norm[:20]

# find entries where both normalized tokens 'اخوان' and 'الكهف' appear
hits_both = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ""
    cand_norm = normalize_arabic(cand)
    if 'اخوان' in cand_norm and 'الكهف' in cand_norm:
        hits_both.append((idx, cand[:400], cand_norm[:400]))
len(hits_both)

# Using earlier `matches` results, but reconstruct matches by searching for 'الكهف' raw in cand
matches = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ""
    if 'الكهف' in cand or 'الكهفِ' in cand or 'الْكَهْفِ' in cand:
        matches.append(idx)
len(matches), matches[:50]

for idx in matches:
    entry = data_h[idx]
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ""
    cand_norm = normalize_arabic(cand)
    print("Index:", idx)
    print("Normalized snippet:", cand_norm[:400])
    print("Original snippet:", cand[:400])
    print("----------\n")

# Search for normalized 'لم يفن' occurrences
term = normalize_arabic("لَمْ يُفَنَّ")
hits = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ""
    if 'لم يفن' in normalize_arabic(cand):
        hits.append((idx, cand[:400], normalize_arabic(cand)[:400]))
len(hits), hits[:20]

# search for 'اقاويلنا' or 'اقاويل' normalized
term = normalize_arabic("أَقاوِيلِنَا")
hits = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ""
    cand_norm = normalize_arabic(cand)
    if 'اقاويل' in cand_norm or 'اقاويلنا' in cand_norm or 'باقاويلنا' in cand_norm or 'يءمن باقاويلنا' in cand_norm:
        hits.append((idx, cand[:400], cand_norm[:400]))
len(hits), hits[:30]

# search for 'لم يؤمن' words normalized
hits = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    cand_norm = normalize_arabic(entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or "")
    if 'لم يامن' in cand_norm or 'لم يامن' in cand_norm.replace('ء',''):
        hits.append((idx, cand_norm[:200]))
len(hits), hits[:20]

# Compute combined similarity for all entries: max(token_f1, lcs)
best = []
for idx, entry in enumerate(data_h):
    cand = entry.get('Matn') or entry.get('hadithTxt') or ""
    cand_norm = normalize_arabic(cand)
    f1 = token_f1(q_norm, cand_norm)
    lcs = lcs_char_ratio(q_norm, cand_norm)
    score = max(f1, lcs)
    best.append((idx, score, f1, lcs, cand[:200]))
# sort
best_sorted = sorted(best, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
best_sorted[:30]
